items.title: Modi urges Indians to get rid of foreign products
items.description: STORY: :: India's Modi asks citizens to stop using foreign-made products and instead to buy local:: September 21, 2025:: Narendra Modi, Indian Prime Minister"We are using many foreign goods in our day-to-day life, knowingly or unknowingly. We don't even know if the comb in our pocket is locally-made or foreign-made, we don't know. We also have to get rid of it. We will buy goods that are made in India."After U.S. President Donald Trump imposed a 50% tariff on imported Indian goods, Modi has been urging use of "Swadeshi", or made-in-India goods. His supporters have started campaigns to boycott American brands including McDonald's, Pepsi and Apple, which are hugely popular in India.India's population of 1.4 billion is a major market for American consumer goods, often purchased from U.S. online retailer Amazon.com. Over the years, the reach of U.S. brands has expanded deep into smaller towns.

items.title: Is Having a Position in the Entire Stock Market a Better Option Than Investing in the S&P 500?
items.description: The S&P 500 continues to hit new records, and tracking it may no longer be the safest option for risk-averse investors.
